title: The industrial specification form reduced to pure geometry. A white-ground composition with a black header band, framing borders, and a central box containing the `->` operator. The form IS a specification sheet: the header identifies the category, the frame bounds the governed domain, the central box is the specified operation. No text needed — the structural arrangement communicates 'this is a formal declaration.'
conceptualBridge: The specification form means Precept's definition is a formal declaration because the framed structure with a header identifies the contract the way an engineering spec sheet identifies its subject — bounded, categorized, and centered on the one thing it specifies.
decision: Shortlisted: 4.5 avg — the abstract specification form works without text; white-background industrial aesthetic is completely new to the set; the white/black contrast is maximum; at 32px the header band + central box reads as a framed formal declaration. Near the top of the set.
